comments.description: Single-line comments start with double slashes and extend to the end of the line【610611899728558†L43-L47】.

identifiers.description: Identifiers (names for variables, functions, etc.) may contain letters, digits and underscores; they cannot start with a digit, cannot exceed 63 characters, and must not be reserved words【413453431039501†L43-L49】.

1‑byte signed integer type with range ‑128 to 127【768075811836515†L46-L170】.

1‑byte unsigned integer type with range 0 to 255【768075811836515†L46-L170】.

2‑byte unsigned integer type with range 0 to 65535【768075811836515†L46-L170】.

Sequence of characters forming a text string; can vary in length and supports standard string operations.
Indicates that a function does not return a value.

access_specifiers.description: Qualifies a variable or parameter as constant so that its value cannot be changed after initialization【539297262116978†L117-L149】.

operators.description: Terminates the nearest enclosing loop or switch and transfers control to the statement following the loop or switch【786933659818190†L52-L118】.
Performs a safe type cast of pointers or references within an inheritance hierarchy.
Exits a function and optionally returns a value to the caller【731200081161575†L52-L83】.

Returns the size, in bytes, of a data type or variable.
Skips the remaining statements in the current loop iteration and begins the next iteration【786933659818190†L52-L118】.
Loop construct that repeats a block of code for a specified number of iterations; consists of initialization, condition and iteration expressions【786933659818190†L52-L118】.

Loop construct that repeats a block while a condition remains true【786933659818190†L52-L118】.
Deallocates memory previously allocated with new, destroying the object.
Allocates memory for an object and returns a pointer to it.

Keyword used when overloading an operator in a class.

arithmetic.description: Subtraction or unary minus; subtracts one operand from another or negates a value【28449630635833†L48-L64】.
Division; divides the left operand by the right operand【28449630635833†L48-L64】.
Modulo; yields the remainder after dividing the left operand by the right operand【28449630635833†L48-L64】.

Decrement operator; decreases a variable by 1; prefix or postfix forms【28449630635833†L48-L64】.

assignment.description: Assigns the value of the right operand to the left operand.
Adds the right operand to the left operand and assigns the result to the left operand【687789900525068†L51-L67】.
Subtracts the right operand from the left operand and assigns the result to the left operand【687789900525068†L51-L67】.
Multiplies the left operand by the right operand and assigns the result to the left operand【687789900525068†L51-L67】.
Divides the left operand by the right operand and assigns the result to the left operand【687789900525068†L51-L67】.
Assigns the remainder of dividing the left operand by the right operand to the left operand【687789900525068†L51-L67】.
Right‑shift assignment; shifts the left operand right by the number of bits specified by the right operand and assigns the result.
Left‑shift assignment; shifts the left operand left by the number of bits specified by the right operand and assigns the result.
Bitwise AND assignment; performs bitwise AND on the operands and assigns the result.
Bitwise OR assignment; performs bitwise OR on the operands and assigns the result.
Bitwise XOR assignment; performs bitwise XOR on the operands and assigns the result.

relational.description: Equality comparison; returns true if operands are equal【818006071481343†L48-L60】.
Inequality comparison; returns true if operands are not equal【818006071481343†L48-L60】.
Less‑than comparison; returns true if left operand is less than right operand【818006071481343†L48-L60】.
Greater‑than comparison; returns true if left operand is greater than right operand【818006071481343†L48-L60】.

logical.description: Logical NOT; returns true if operand is false and false if operand is true【894695048909558†L48-L66】.
Logical OR; returns true if either operand is true; evaluation is short‑circuited【894695048909558†L48-L66】.
Logical AND; returns true only if both operands are true; evaluation is short‑circuited【894695048909558†L48-L66】.

bitwise.description: Bitwise NOT; inverts each bit of its operand【562041208282356†L48-L103】.
Right shift; shifts bits of the left operand right by a specified number; sign bit is preserved for signed types【562041208282356†L48-L103】.
Left shift; shifts bits of the left operand left by a specified number【562041208282356†L48-L103】.
Bitwise AND; performs logical AND on each pair of bits of the operands.
Bitwise OR; performs logical inclusive OR on each pair of bits of the operands.
Bitwise XOR; performs logical exclusive OR on each pair of bits of the operands.

variables.description: Variables must be declared with a type before use; declaration syntax is: type name [= initial_value]; multiple variables can be declared in one statement【539297262116978†L48-L66】.
Arrays are sequences of elements of the same type; up to four dimensions are allowed; indexing starts at 0 and ends at size ‑ 1【539297262116978†L92-L113】.
Using const before a variable declaration makes the variable read‑only; its value cannot change after initialization【539297262116978†L117-L149】.
static keyword declares variables with static storage duration; they retain their value between function calls and are initialized once【539297262116978†L117-L149】.
extern keyword declares a variable defined in another module; allows sharing global variables among modules【539297262116978†L117-L149】.

Specifies program properties such as version, copyright, description and icon【436213582272699†L42-L62】.

functions.description: A function definition consists of a return type, function name and parameter list followed by a body enclosed in braces; functions can be called multiple times and help structure code【731200081161575†L52-L83】.
A function can have up to 64 parameters; each parameter has a type and name; parameters are passed by value unless specified otherwise【731200081161575†L52-L83】.

Multiple functions can share the same name provided they have different parameter lists, enabling polymorphism.

object_oriented.description: Class defines a new data type that encapsulates variables and functions; instances of classes are objects【973481063463211†L47-L60】.
Combining data and methods in one unit (class) to model real‑world objects【973481063463211†L47-L60】.
Mechanism by which a class (derived class) can acquire the properties and behaviour of another class (base class)【973481063463211†L47-L60】.
